annotations.label: de Rham cohomology of the circle

anchor.snippet: A simple example is the de Rham cohomology of the circle.

annotations.note: Mathlib has no de Rham cohomology of manifolds (the only `deRham` hits are in `Mathlib.RingTheory.Perfectoid.BDeRham`, an unrelated p-adic period ring).

annotations.label: Cochain complex and cohomology group

anchor.snippet: Cohomology is often expressed by a cochain complex

annotations.note: `CochainComplex` is defined in `Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.HomologicalComplex` and `HomologicalComplex.homology` computes the cohomology groups.

annotations.label: Cocycles, coboundaries, cohomology classes

anchor.snippet: are called cocycles

annotations.note: Cocycles are defined as `CochainComplex.HomComplex.Cocycle`, with `HomologicalComplex.cycles`/`homology` providing the general cycles, boundaries, and cohomology-class machinery.

annotations.label: Singular cohomology

anchor.snippet: the definition of singular cohomology starts with the singular chain complex

annotations.note: The singular chain complex and singular homology functor are formalized, but the dual cochain-complex/cohomology version is not exposed under a `SingularCohomology` name.

annotations.label: Homotopy invariance of cohomology

anchor.snippet: Two homotopic maps

annotations.note: Homotopic continuous maps induce equal maps on singular homology, proved in the singular homology homotopy-invariance file.

annotations.label: Mayer–Vietoris long exact sequence

anchor.snippet: The Mayer–Vietoris sequence is an important computational tool

annotations.note: Mayer–Vietoris exists for sheaf cohomology via Mayer–Vietoris squares, but the topological singular cohomology version is not formalized.

annotations.label: Relative cohomology long exact sequence

anchor.snippet: There are relative cohomology groups

annotations.note: The `HomologyPretheory` structure axiomatizes relative homology with boundary maps δ, but no concrete relative singular cohomology long exact sequence is proved.

annotations.label: Universal coefficient theorem

anchor.snippet: The universal coefficient theorem describes cohomology in terms of homology

annotations.note: No `universalCoefficient`/UCT statement found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Vanishing of cohomology above dimension

anchor.snippet: is a topological manifold or a CW complex

annotations.note: No formalized statement about vanishing of H^i above the dimension of a manifold or CW complex was found.

annotations.label: Finite generation of cohomology for compact manifolds

anchor.snippet: is a compact manifold (possibly with boundary)

annotations.note: Mathlib has no theorem asserting finite generation of singular cohomology of compact manifolds.

annotations.label: Cup product

anchor.snippet: there is a bilinear map , called the cup product

annotations.note: Grep and loogle for `cup`/`cupProduct` found nothing related to cohomology cup products.

annotations.label: Graded-commutativity of cup product

anchor.snippet: It is graded-commutative in the sense that

annotations.note: No cup product is formalized in Mathlib, hence no graded-commutativity lemma.

annotations.label: Pullback is a homomorphism of graded algebras

anchor.snippet: For any continuous map

annotations.note: Without a cohomology ring, the statement that f* is a graded-algebra map is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cup product as intersection of submanifolds

anchor.snippet: the cup product describes the intersection of submanifolds

annotations.note: No formalization of cup product as intersection of submanifolds in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Thom's pseudomanifold representation

anchor.snippet: every integral cohomology class on a manifold can be represented by a

annotations.note: Thom's pseudomanifold representability theorem is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: de Rham's theorem

anchor.snippet: de Rham's theorem says that the singular cohomology

annotations.note: Mathlib lacks de Rham cohomology of manifolds and hence the comparison with singular cohomology.

annotations.label: Cohomology ring of a point

anchor.snippet: The cohomology ring of a point is the ring

annotations.note: No cohomology ring is formalized; only `singularHomologyFunctor` of disconnected/point spaces gives the group, not the ring structure.

annotations.label: Cohomology ring of the sphere

anchor.snippet: the cohomology ring of the sphere

annotations.note: No formalization of H*(S^n) as a graded ring was found.

annotations.label: Cohomology ring of the torus

anchor.snippet: The cohomology ring of the torus

annotations.note: Cohomology rings are absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Künneth formula

anchor.snippet: the Künneth formula gives that the cohomology ring of the product space

annotations.note: Grep for `Kunneth`/`Künneth` found no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Cohomology ring of real projective space

anchor.snippet: The cohomology ring of real projective space

annotations.note: Cohomology ring of RP^n is not formalized.

annotations.label: Cohomology ring of complex projective space

anchor.snippet: The cohomology ring of complex projective space

annotations.note: Cohomology ring of CP^n is not formalized.

annotations.label: Cohomology ring of a closed oriented surface

anchor.snippet: The cohomology ring of the closed oriented surface

annotations.note: Cohomology ring of closed oriented surfaces is not formalized.

annotations.label: Odd-degree classes square to zero (over 1/2-rings)

anchor.snippet: graded-commutativity of the cohomology ring implies that

annotations.note: Without a cohomology ring/cup product, this consequence of graded-commutativity is not present.

annotations.label: Cup product from the diagonal map

anchor.snippet: The cup product on cohomology can be viewed as coming from the diagonal map

annotations.note: No diagonal-map definition of cup product on topological cohomology is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: External (cross) product

anchor.snippet: there is an external product (or cross product ) cohomology class

annotations.note: Only unrelated `crossProduct`/`externalProduct` definitions exist (linear algebra, monoidal categories); no cohomological cross product.

annotations.label: Poincaré duality (self-duality of cohomology ring)

anchor.snippet: the cohomology ring of a closed oriented manifold is self-dual in a strong sense

annotations.note: Mathlib has no Poincaré duality theorem (no `Poincare`/`PoincareDuality` decl on manifolds).

annotations.label: Euler class of an oriented vector bundle

anchor.snippet: An oriented real vector bundle

annotations.note: No `EulerClass`/`eulerClass` decl exists for oriented vector bundles in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Eilenberg–MacLane space

anchor.snippet: Such a space is called an Eilenberg–MacLane space

annotations.note: Grep and loogle for `EilenbergMacLane` returned nothing.

annotations.label: Eilenberg–MacLane spaces classify cohomology

anchor.snippet: it is a classifying space for cohomology

annotations.note: Eilenberg–MacLane spaces and their representability of cohomology are not formalized.

annotations.label: First cohomology classifies principal A-bundles

anchor.snippet: There is a related description of the first cohomology

annotations.note: No statement classifying principal bundles via H^1(X;A) was found.

annotations.label: Cap product

anchor.snippet: the cap product is a bilinear map

annotations.note: No `capProduct`/`CapProduct` declaration exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fundamental class and Poincaré duality via cap product

anchor.snippet: A closed oriented manifold X of dimension n has a fundamental class

annotations.note: No `FundamentalClass` of a closed oriented manifold is formalized.

annotations.label: Sheaf cohomology as right derived functors

anchor.snippet: Grothendieck defined sheaf cohomology groups to be the right derived functors

annotations.note: `Sheaf.H F n` is defined as `Ext` from the constant sheaf ℤ to F, equivalent to the right-derived-functor definition for sites with enough injectives.

annotations.label: Sheaf cohomology as Ext

anchor.snippet: Sheaf cohomology can be identified with a type of Ext group

annotations.note: `Sheaf.H` is by definition an `Ext`-group, so the identification is built in.

annotations.label: l-adic cohomology

anchor.snippet: This is defined as the projective limit

annotations.note: Defined as pro-étale sheaf cohomology with coefficients in ℤ_ℓ, equivalent (mod the classical comparison) to the projective limit definition.

annotations.label: Comparison of Betti and l-adic dimensions

anchor.snippet: then there is an equality of dimensions for the Betti cohomology

annotations.note: Comparison between Betti and ℓ-adic cohomology dimensions is not formalized.

annotations.label: Blowup long exact sequence

anchor.snippet: Another useful computational tool is the blowup sequence

annotations.note: No blowup long exact sequence in cohomology found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Eilenberg–Steenrod axioms

anchor.snippet: there is a list of properties known as the Eilenberg–Steenrod axioms

annotations.note: `HomologyPretheory` bundles the data of a (relative) homology theory with boundary maps and a homotopy-invariance typeclass `IsHomotopyInvariant`; the full axiom suite (exactness, excision, dimension) is not yet packaged.

annotations.label: Generalized homology theory

anchor.snippet: a generalized homology theory is a sequence of functors

annotations.note: `HomologyPretheory` is parametrized by a `ComplexShape` and so models the sequence-of-functors data of a generalized homology theory, though without the dimension axiom.

annotations.label: Generalized cohomology theory

anchor.snippet: a generalized cohomology theory is a sequence of contravariant functors

annotations.note: The dual `CohomologyPretheory` (contravariant) is not present in Mathlib; only the covariant `HomologyPretheory` exists.

annotations.label: Brown representability for (co)homology theories

anchor.snippet: every generalized homology theory comes from a spectrum

annotations.note: Brown representability is not formalized; no `brownRepresentability` decl was found.

annotations.label: Multiplicative cohomology theory

anchor.snippet: A cohomology theory E is said to be multiplicative

annotations.note: Multiplicative (ring) structure on generalized cohomology theories is not formalized.
